arabic_text:
مواد قدسی
قلم (عقل أول)
لوح (منبعث أول)
لا اله الا الله
عقول
alt_text: Schematic illustration of the cosmological writing metaphor (دوات قلم اور لوح — Inkwell, Pen, and Tablet). Upper right: an inkwell labeled مواد قدسی (sacred materials) in red. Upper left: a pen labeled قلم (عقل أول) — Pen (First Intellect) — in red. Center: a black tablet labeled لوح (منبعث أول) — Tablet (First Emanator) — with the Kalima لا اله الا الله (There is no god but Allah) inscribed on it in white calligraphy. Bottom: an arrow pointing downward to عقول (the Intellects), depicting the hierarchical descent from the divine source through the First Intellect and First Emanator down to the subordinate intellects. Matches the topic دوات قلم اور لوح.
notes: The kalima لا اله الا الله is the Islamic creedal formula (Shahada), not a single Quran verse. The Quranic phrasing closest to it appears in Surah 3:18, 37:35, 47:19 and others, but in those contexts it is part of a longer ayat. Do NOT cite a specific (surah, ayat) for this image — the kalima here is doctrinal text, not a quoted verse.
